title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Jackson Heights, Queens
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Jackson Heights, Queens. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Licensed experts. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: Jackson Heights residents dealing with bats in their attics, chimneys, or wall voids need professional bat removal services that are both humane and effective. Our bat exclusion specialists serve the historic garden apartments, co-ops, and single-family homes throughout Jackson Heights with proven methods that safely remove bats and prevent their return. With decades of experience in Queens bat control, we understand the unique challenges posed by the diverse architecture of this vibrant neighborhood.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has been serving Jackson Heights for over 20 years, providing licensed bat removal services that comply with all New York State wildlife regulations. Our technicians are trained in humane exclusion techniques and use only proven methods to protect your property while ensuring bats are safely relocated without harm.
localContext: Jackson Heights' pre-war garden apartments, Tudor-style homes, and multi-family buildings along Northern Boulevard and Roosevelt Avenue often provide ideal entry points for bats seeking shelter. The tree-lined streets near Travers Park and the historic district's older structures require specialized bat exclusion techniques that preserve architectural integrity while ensuring complete protection.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Jackson Heights?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Jackson Heights typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your building's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Jackson Heights, Queens?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and in compliance with New York State regulations. Bats are protected species, so removal can only be performed during specific seasons (typically late summer/early fall) when young bats are able to fly. Our licensed professionals ensure all work meets legal requirements.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Jackson Heights home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around small openings, and seeing bats flying around your property at twilight. Bats can enter through openings as small as 3/8 of an inch, common in older Jackson Heights buildings.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive bat exclusion includes a thorough inspection to identify all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing of all potential entry points, attic cleanup and sanitization if needed, and a warranty to guarantee bats won't return. The process typically takes 2-4 weeks to ensure all bats have safely exited.
